top_signals.title: Honda R&D’s Yoshiike on Global Robotics Competition
top_signals.summary:
Takahide Yoshiike, Executive Chief Engineer for Honda’s R&D Frontier Robotics division, notes that while global competition in robotics is intense, each region has distinct strengths. Chinese companies excel at reducing production costs for humanoid robots, the United States leads in AI chip development, and Japan’s advantage lies in manufacturing precise, high-quality hardware.
He also discusses the evolution of Honda’s humanoid robotics efforts. He speaks with Shery Ahn from the sidelines of the Tokyo Humanoids Summit on "Bloomberg Tech Asia". (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Google Deepmind's Parada on Robotics Technology
top_signals.summary: Carolina Parada, VP and Head of Robotics at Google Deepmind, says “embodied intelligence is the next frontier of artificial intelligence.” She is focused on pushing the boundaries of robots’ ability to perform highly dexterous tasks, such as folding origami or packing a lunch box. Parada also discusses the development and research direction of Gemini Robotics. She speaks with Shery Ahn on the sidelines of the Tokyo Humanoids Summit on "Bloomberg Tech Asia". (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
